tex:
(a) The table shows the temperature at Lexford Station at 10 00 each day for a week.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Day & Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri & Sat & Sun \\
\hline
Temperature ($^\circ$C) & -3 & 4 & -1 & 0 & -5 & 2 & 1 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) (b) Write down the day which had the coldest temperature.

(c) Work out the difference in the temperature between Monday and Tuesday.

$^\circ$C

(d) The temperature falls 6$^\circ$C from 10 00 to midnight on Sunday.

Work out the temperature at midnight.

$^\circ$C

(e) The distance between Lexford Station and Crowton Station is 6.5 km.

(i) (f) A train travels between these stations at an average speed of 39 km/h.

Work out how long, in minutes, it takes the train to travel between these stations.

 min

(g) Each wheel on the train has a diameter of 1.8 m.

Work out the number of complete turns each wheel makes in travelling the 6.5 km.

(h) A northbound train leaves Lexford Station every 30 minutes.
A bus leaves Lexford Station every 45 minutes.

At 11 40 a northbound train and a bus leave the station together.

Find the next time when this happens.

(i) Here is part of a timetable for trains going east to west from Lexford Station.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
& Lexford & 09 14 & 09 47 & 10 21 & 11 15 & 11 48 \\
\hline
& Crowton & 09 26 & 09 59 & 10 33 & 11 27 & 12 00 \\
\hline
& Doniton Halt & 09 42 & 10 15 & 10 49 & 11 43 & 12 16 \\
\hline
& Mosshead & 10 01 & 10 34 & 11 08 & 12 02 & 12 35 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) (j) Work out the number of minutes the 09 14 train takes to travel from Lexford to Mosshead.

 min

(k) Freda must arrive at Mosshead by 11 30.

Write down the latest time she can catch a train from Lexford.

(l) 437 people go on a coach trip.
Each coach seats 62 people.

How many coaches are needed?

(a) A museum's opening times are shown in this table.

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Day & Opening times \\
\hline
Monday to Thursday & 09 00 to 17 00 \\
\hline
Friday & 08 30 to 18 00 \\
\hline
Saturday & 09 00 to 19 00 \\
\hline
Sunday & Closed \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Work out how many hours in a week the museum is open for.

 hours

(b) The table shows the cost of tickets for the museum.

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
 & Cost \\
\hline
Adult & \$4.20 \\
\hline
Senior (aged over 60) & \$2.80 \\
\hline
Child (aged 5 to 15) & \$1.80 \\
\hline
Child (aged under 5) & Free \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

The Reeve family visit the museum.
Mrs Reeve is aged 36, her father is 67, her mother is 65, and her three children are 2, 7 and 12.

Work out the total cost for these six people to visit the museum.

\$ 

(c) Mrs Reeve buys 6 ice creams.
Each ice cream costs \$1.30.

How much change does she receive from \$10?

\$ 

(d) Last year, the museum had twenty seven thousand and fifty three visitors.

Write this number in figures.

(e) In 2015, there were 12 400 visitors to the museum.
In 2016, there were 14 100 visitors to the museum.

Calculate the percentage increase in the number of visitors from 2015 to 2016.

 \%

(f) The door to the museum has an 8-digit code to unlock it.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Digit & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 & 7 & 8 \\
\hline
Code & 3 & 7 &  &  &  &  &  &  \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

• The next odd number after 35 gives digits 1 and 2. \\
• The next prime number after 23 gives digits 3 and 4. \\
• The square root of 225 gives digits 5 and 6. \\
• The value of 26 gives digits 7 and 8.

Use this information to complete the door code.
Digits 1 and 2 have been completed for you.

(a) Geoff keeps a record of the number of goals scored in the first eight games played by his football team.

3 \quad 1 \quad 8 \quad 5 \quad 7 \quad 2 \quad 1 \quad 6

Find

(i) (b) the mode,

(c) the range,

(d) the median.

(e) The table shows the number of goals scored by Geoff's team in each game during one season.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Number of goals & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 & 7 & 8 \\
\hline
Number of games & 5 & 7 & 8 & 10 & 6 & 4 & 5 & 3 & 2 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) (f) How many games did the team play?

(g) Work out the mean number of goals scored per game.

(h) Geoff asks some supporters to choose a new colour for the team's shirts.
The results are to be shown in a pie chart.
The table shows some of this information.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Colour & Frequency & Pie chart sector angle \\
\hline
Red & 41 & 123$^\circ$ \\
\hline
Blue &  & 69$^\circ$ \\
\hline
Green &  &  \\
\hline
Other & 18 & 54$^\circ$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) (i) Complete the table.

(j) Complete the pie chart.

(a) A bag contains 6 green balls, 5 red balls and 3 blue balls only.
A ball is taken from the bag at random.

Find the probability that the ball is

(i) (b) green,

(c) green or red,

(d) yellow.

(e) Another bag contains brown balls, white balls, black balls and purple balls only.
A ball is taken from this bag at random.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Colour & Brown & White & Black & Purple \\
\hline
Probability & 0.46 & 0.22 & 0.14 &  \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(i) (f) Complete the table.

(g) Which colour is the most likely to be taken?

(h) There are 50 balls in this bag.

Work out the number of black balls.

8, \quad 15, \quad 22, \quad 29

(i) (b) Find the next term of this sequence.

(c) Describe the rule for continuing this sequence.

(d) Find an expression for the nth term of this sequence.

(e) Find the first three terms of another sequence whose nth term is $n^2 + 10$.

 ,  ,
